sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content: [{"pathway_id": "potentially_resectable_or_transplantable", "title": "Potentially Resectable or Transplantable HCC", "branching_basis": ["Tumor burden resectability", "Transplant criteria eligibility (UNOS)", "Performance status and comorbidities", "Liver function (CTP class)", "Presence of portal hypertension"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "Potentially resectable or transplantable by tumor burden; and operable by performance status or comorbidity. Undergoes multidisciplinary evaluation to assess liver reserve and comorbidity, and staging workup (HCC-3).", "options": [{"type": "Surgery", "label": "Resection", "preference": "Preferred if resection criteria are met (CTP Class A, no portal hypertension, suitable tumor location, adequate liver reserve and remnant).", "recommendation": "Liver resection is a potentially curative option. In highly selected patients with CTP Class B, limited resection may be considered [HCC-4, HCC-F].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "Surgery", "label": "Transplantation", "preference": "Preferred if transplant criteria are met (UNOS criteria). Refer to liver transplant center. Bridge therapy as indicated.", "recommendation": "Liver transplantation is a potentially curative option for early HCC with cirrhosis, treating both tumor and underlying liver disease [HCC-4, HCC-F].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "Locoregional Therapy", "label": "Ablation", "preference": "Preferred locoregional therapy for small, properly located tumors in patients not candidates for surgery.", "recommendation": "Ablation (microwave/radiofrequency or percutaneous ethanol injection) should be considered as definitive curative-intent treatment in well-selected patients with tumors ≤3 cm when performed at experienced centers [HCC-4, HCC-G].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "Locoregional Therapy", "label": "Arterially Directed Therapies or Radiation Therapy (RT)", "preference": "Other locoregional options.", "recommendation": "Arterially directed therapies or RT may be considered based on disease characteristics and expertise [HCC-4].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "Systemic Therapy", "label": "Systemic Therapy", "preference": "Not first-line for this curable stage but may be considered for specific situations.", "recommendation": "Systemic therapy may be considered if deemed ineligible for transplant and with unresectable disease after locoregional therapy assessment, or for high-risk features warranting adjuvant consideration (though adjuvant data is evolving) [HCC-4].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": "Yes, for high-risk features after curative-intent therapy.", "summary": "For patients who undergo curative-intent resection or ablation, the role of adjuvant therapy is evolving. The IMbrave050 trial showed improved recurrence-free survival with atezolizumab + bevacizumab vs. active surveillance, but an updated analysis showed the benefit was not sustained; thus, it is not included as a standard option [MS-41]. Adjuvant antiviral therapy is recommended for patients with viral hepatitis to reduce recurrence [HCC-F, MS-41]. Post-resection, surveillance with cross-sectional imaging and AFP every 3-6 months for 2 years, then every 6 months is recommended [HCC-4]."}}]}, {"pathway_id": "liver_confined_unresectable_ineligible_transplant", "title": "Liver-Confined, Unresectable, Ineligible for Transplant", "branching_basis": ["Tumor burden", "Liver function (CTP class, portal hypertension)", "Performance status", "Access to locoregional therapies", "Prior locoregional therapy response"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "Liver-confined, unresectable HCC, deemed ineligible for transplant due to inadequate hepatic reserve, tumor location, or extent of disease.", "options": [{"type": "Locoregional Therapy", "label": "Ablation, Arterially Directed Therapies, or RT", "preference": "Locoregional therapies are the primary approach. Choice depends on extent/location, hepatic reserve, and institutional capabilities.", "recommendation": "Ablation is preferred for small tumors. Arterially directed therapies (TACE, TAE, radioembolization) are options for tumors not amenable to ablation. RT (including SBRT) is a treatment option, especially when other therapies have failed or are contraindicated [HCC-5, HCC-G, HCC-H].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "Systemic Therapy", "label": "Systemic Therapy", "preference": "An option, particularly if there is progression on or after locoregional therapy.", "recommendation": "Systemic therapy should be considered. Preferred first-line regimens include atezolizumab + bevacizumab, durvalumab + tremelimumab-actl, or ipilimumab + nivolumab [HCC-I, HCC-5].", "linked_regimen_ids": ["atezolizumab_bevacizumab", "durvalumab_tremelimumab", "ipilimumab_nivolumab"]}, {"type": "Other", "label": "Clinical Trial or Best Supportive Care", "preference": "Appropriate options at any point.", "recommendation": "Clinical trial enrollment is encouraged. Best supportive care is an option for patients who are not candidates for or decline other treatments [HCC-I, HCC-5].", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": null}]}]

nodes.label: Potentially resectable or transplantable by tumor burden; and operable by performance status or comorbidity. Undergoes multidisciplinary evaluation to assess liver reserve and comorbidity, and staging workup (HCC-3).
Arterially Directed Therapies or Radiation Therapy (RT)

edges.label: Preferred if resection criteria are met (CTP Class A, no portal hypertension, suitable tumor location, adequate liver reserve and remnant).
Preferred if transplant criteria are met (UNOS criteria). Refer to liver transplant center. Bridge therapy as indicated.
Preferred locoregional therapy for small, properly located tumors in patients not candidates for surgery.
Not first-line for this curable stage but may be considered for specific situations.

nodes.label: Liver-confined, unresectable HCC, deemed ineligible for transplant due to inadequate hepatic reserve, tumor location, or extent of disease.
Ablation, Arterially Directed Therapies, or RT
Clinical Trial or Best Supportive Care

edges.label: Locoregional therapies are the primary approach. Choice depends on extent/location, hepatic reserve, and institutional capabilities.
An option, particularly if there is progression on or after locoregional therapy.
